---
title: "Mangrove Biomass Equations"
format: html
execute:
  eval: false
---

## Purpose

Explain how the four `dina` mangrove biomass equations are ingested, joined to
wood density, and made available to `miaproc.biomass`.

Source script:

- `../mia-allometric/R/04_ingest_biomass.R`

Python downstream package reference:

- `../miaproc/src/miaproc/biomass/data/equation_application_unified.zstd.parquet`

The source paths assume sibling repositories. Replace `../mia-allometric` or
`../miaproc` with your local source-repo paths if your checkout differs.

```{python}
from miaproc.biomass import load_packaged_equations

eq = load_packaged_equations()
dina = eq[eq["source_dataset"].eq("dina")]
dina[[
    "source_record_id",
    "scientific_name_apg_raw",
    "wood_density_value",
    "equation_numpy",
    "equation_numpy_wd_fixed",
    "response_variable",
    "response_units",
]]
```

## What to explain

- The raw source equation is preserved.
- `DAP` / `DBH` is normalized to `diam`.
- `WD` is kept as `wd` in `equation_numpy`.
- `equation_numpy_wd_fixed` substitutes the species wood-density literal.
- The direct biomass response is `B` in `kg`.

```{bash}
cd <path-to-mia-allometric>
Rscript R/04_ingest_biomass.R
```
